hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1003   -  وَّ رَّوَّى جَّزِي ز   عَّن ْ ر ُّرَّإرَّة َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ ج َّهَّزَّ ق ِي مَّا لأَّ ي َّن ْن َّع ِى  إَّلْح َّهْزُّ ق ِي هِ إ َّوْ إ َّج ْق َّى  
ق ِي مَّا لأَّ ي َّن ْن َّع ِى  إَّلْأ ِج ْق َّاءُّ ق ِي هِ ق َّق َّال عَّاذَّة ُّ وَّ إ ِن ْ ق َّعَّلَّ ذ َّلِك َّ ن َّاسِي اً إ َّوْ 
سَّاهِي اً إ َّوْ لأَّ   ي َّذْرِى  ق َّلاَّ س َّى ْءَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ ق َّذْ ي َّم َّث ْ صَّلاَّي ُّه  ق َّذ َّكَّزَّهَّا ق ِى  
إَّلْأ َّج ِي زَّي َّي ْن ِ ق َّق َّال  وَّ إَّلي  َّكْن ِي زَّ وَّ إَّلي  َّسْن ِي حَّ إَّل َّذ ِى  ق َّاي َّهُّ ق ِى  إَّلْأ َّو َّلَّي َّي ْن ِ ق ِى  إَّلْأ َّج ِي زَّي َّي ْن ِ وَّ لأَّ س َّى ْءَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ .
hadiths.en:
Zurarah narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} that He said:  
"When you say the Takbir at the beginning of your prayer after the opening Takbir 
(Takbir al-Ihram), if you say twenty-one Takbirs and then forget the remaining Takbirs, 
or fail to say them, the first Takbir will suffice for all the Takbirs in the prayer”.   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 343 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 144 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 
8, p. 642 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 19 • Al-Fusul al-Muhimmah, Part 2, p. 92 • Bihar Al-Anwar, V. 81, p. 378 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 1002

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1004 - وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ُّ ن ْن ُّ جَّم َّاذٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ لَّهُّ إ َّسْهُّو عَّن ِ إَّلْق ِزَّإءَّة ِ ق ِى  إَّلز َّكْعَّه ِ إَّلْأ ُّولَّى  
ق َّال هُّو ق ِى  صَّلاَّي ِى  كُّلِّهَّا ق َّق َّالَّ  
«كُّوعَّ وَّ إَّلس ُّح ُّوذَّ ق َّق َّذْ ي َّم َّث ْ صَّلاَّي ُّك .
hadiths.en:
Hariz narrated from Zurarah, from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} 
regarding a man who recites aloud (Jahr) where it should not be done, or recites 
silently (ikhfāʾ) where it should be recited aloud.  
Imam {a.s} said: "Whoever does this deliberately has invalidated his prayer and must 
repeat it. However, if he does so out of forgetfulness, inattentiveness, or without 
knowing, there is no penalty on him, and his prayer remains valid."  
Zurarah then asked: "What if a person forgets to recite in the first two rak ’ahs (units of 
prayer) but remembers in the latter two?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "He should make up the recitation, takbir (saying 'Allāhu Akbar'), and 
tasbih that he missed in the first two rak ’ahs during the latter two, and there is no 
penalty on him."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 1003

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1005   -  وَّ رَّوَّى ر ُّرَّإرَّة ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّجَّذِهِمَّا عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال وي َّعَّالَّى  ق َّزَّض َّ إَّلز ُّكُّوعَّ وَّ إَّلس ُّح ُّوذَّ وَّ إَّلْق ِزَّإءَّة ُّ 
سُّن  َّه   ق َّمَّن ْ ي َّزَّك َّ إَّلْق ِزَّإءَّة َّ مُّي َّعَّمِّذإً إ َّعَّاذَّ إَّلص َّلاَّة َّ وَّ مَّن ْ ن َّسِى َّ ق َّلاَّ س َّى ْءَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ .
hadiths.en:
Hussain ibn Hammad narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} 
that a man said to him: "I forget the recitation in the first rak'ah."  
Imam {a.s} said: "Recite in the second."  
The man then said: "I forget in the second rak'ah as well."  
Imam {a.s} said: "Recite in the third."  
The man said: "I forget throughout my entire prayer."  
Imam {a.s} said: "If you have remembered the ruku' (bowing) and Sujood (prostration), 
then your prayer is complete." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 344 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 148 • Al -Istibsar, 
Part 1, p. 355 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 3, p. 91 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 922 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 93 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 1004

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1006  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْعَّلاَّءُّ عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ مُّسْلِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ س َّك  َّ ن َّعْذَّ مَّا سَّح َّذَّ إ َّن  َّهُّ لَّمْ ي َّزْكَّغْ 
ق َّق َّال   إَّلس َّح ْذَّي َّي ْن ِ إَّلل َّي َّي ْن ِ لأَّ رُّكُّوعَّ  
لَّهُّمَّا وَّ ي َّن ْن ِى  عَّلَّى صَّلاَّ ي ِهِ إَّل َّي ِى  عَّلَّى إَّلي  َّمَّامِ ق َّا ِن ْ كَّان َّ لَّمْ ي َّسْي َّي ْق ِن ْ إ ِلأ َّ مِن ْ ن َّعْذِ مَّا ق َّزَّع َّ وَّ إِن ْصَّزَّف َّ ق َّلْي َّق ُّمْ وَّ لْي ُّ صَّلِّ رَّكْعَّه ً 
وَّ سَّح ْذَّي َّي ْن ِ وَّ   لأَّ س َّى ْءَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ .
hadiths.en:
Zurarah narrated from one of the Imams that He said:  
"Indeed, Allah {SWT}, Blessed and Exalted, has made ruku' (bowing) and Sujood 
(prostration) obligatory, while recitation is a Sunnah. Whoever intentionally leaves out 
the recitation must repeat the prayer, but if someone forgets, there is no fault upon 
them." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 347 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 345 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, 
Part 2, p. 146 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 353 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 3, p. 83 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 919 • Wasail Al-
Shia, Vol. 6, p. 87 • Al-Fusul al-Muhimmah, Part 2, p. 94 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 85, p. 142 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 1005

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1007   -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّن ْذُّ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ُّ سِن َّان ٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال ن َّ  إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ رُّكُّوعاً إ َّوْ 
سُّح ُّوذإً إ َّوْ ي َّكْن ِي زإً ي ُّم َّ ذ َّكَّزْب َّ ق َّاق ْض ِ إَّل َّذ ِى  ق َّاي َّك َّ سَّهْوإً .
hadiths.en:
Al-‘Ala' narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim from Imam Al-Baqir {a.s} that He 
said:  
"If a man doubts after prostrating that he has not bowed (rak'a), he should continue 
with his prayer until he is certain that he did not bow. If he becomes certain that he did 
not bow, then he should perform the two prostrations (sajdatayn) that have no bowing 
and complete his prayer as though it were perfect.  
However, if he does not become certain until after he has finished and turned away 
from the prayer, he should stand up and pray one more rak'ah with two prostrations, 
and there is no harm upon him." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 345 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 85, p. 140 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 1006

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1008  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إِن ْن ُّ مُّسْكَّان َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ن َّصِي زٍ ق َّالَّ: سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّم َّن ْ ن َّسِى َّ إ َّن ْ ي سْح ُّذَّ وَّإجِذَّة ً 
ق َّذ َّكَّزَّهَّا وَّ هُّوَّ ق َّان ِم  ق َّال ق َّا ِن ْ كَّان َّ ق َّذْ رَّكَّغَّ ق َّلْي َّمْض ِ عَّلَّى صَّلاَّي ِهِ ق َّا ِذ َّإ إِن ْصَّزَّف َّ ق َّص َّاهَّا 
وَّجْذَّهَّا وَّ لَّي ْسَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ سَّهْو  .
hadiths.en:
Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} 
that He said:  
"If you forget something in the prayer, whether it is bowing, prostration, or takbir, and 
then remember it, make up for what you missed due to forgetfulness”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 346 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 238 • Bihar Al -Anwar, 
Vol. 85, p. 151 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 85, p. 264 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 1007

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.en:
Ibn Muskan narrated from Abu Basir who said:  
"I asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} about someone who forgot to perform 
one prostration and remembered it while standing.  
Imam {a.s} said: 'He should perform it when he remembers, as long as he hasn't yet 
bowed. If he has already bowed, he should continue his prayer and, when he finishes, 
he should perform the missed prostration separately. There is no Sahw (forgetfulness) 
on him.'". 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 346 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 85, p. 144 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 1008

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.en:
Mansour ibn Hazim asked Imam {a.s} about a person who prayed and then 
remembered that he had added an extra prostration.  
Imam {a.s} said: "He does not need to repeat his prayer due to the extra prostration, but 
he must repeat it from the point of the ruku' (bowing)”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 346 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 156 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 
8, p. 933 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 319 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 85, p. 162 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 1009

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1011   -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّلِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ ن ُّعْمَّان َّ إَّلز َّإر ِى  ُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ: كُّن ْث ُّ مَّغَّ إ َّصْحَّاب ٍ لِى  ق ِى  سَّق َّزٍ وَّ إ َّن َّا إ ِمَّامُّهُّمْ ق َّصَّل ي ْث ُّ ن ِهِمُّ إَّلْمَّع ْزِب َّ  
ق َّسَّل َّمْث ُّ ق ِى  إَّلز َّكْعَّي َّي ْن ِ إَّلْأ َّو َّلَّي َّي ْن ِ ق َّق َّالَّ إ َّصْحَّان ِى  إ ِن  َّمَّا صَّل َّي ْث َّ ن ِن َّا رَّكْعَّي َّي ْن ِ  ق َّكَّل َّمْي ُّهُّمْ وَّ كَّل َّمُّون ِى  ق َّق َّالُّوإ إ َّم َّا ن َّحْن ُّ ق َّن ُّعِي ذُّ  
ق َّق ُّلْث ُّ لَّكِن ِّى  لأَّ إ ُّعِي ذُّ وَّ إ ُّي ِم ُّ ن ِزَّكْعَّه ٍ ق َّا َّي ْمَّمْث ُّ ن ِزَّكْعَّه ٍ ي ُّم َّ سِزْن َّا وَّ إ َّي َّي ْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّ لَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ وَّ ذ َّكَّزْب ُّ لَّهُّ إَّل َّذ ِى  كَّان َّ  
مِن ْ إ َّمْزِن َّ ا ق َّق َّالَّ لِى
hadiths.en:
Amir ibn Jadh'ah narrated from Imam {a.s} that He said:  
"If the first two rak'ahs are correct, the whole prayer is correct”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 346 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 977 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
« .

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 1010

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.en:
Ali ibn Nu'man al-Razi narrated that He said:  
I was traveling with some companions, and I was their Imam (leading the prayer). I led 
them in Maghrib prayer and mistakenly ended the prayer after two units (rak'ahs).  
My companions told me that I had only led them in two rak'ahs. We discussed it among 
ourselves, and they decided that they would repeat their prayer.  
I, however, decided not to repeat it but to complete it by adding one more rak'ah. So, I 
completed it with an additional rak'ah, and we then continued our journey.  
Later, I went to Imam Ja'far Al-Sadiq {a.s} and told him about what had happened.  
Imam {a.s} said to me: "You acted more correctly than they did. Only the one who does 
not know what he has prayed needs to repeat (the prayer)”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 347 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 181 • Al -Istibsar, 
Part 1, p. 371 • Nuzhat al-Nāẓir, Part 1, p. 38 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 958 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 199 • Bihar 
Al-Anwar, Vol. 85, p. 198 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 1011

reference.location: Volume 1 › THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٩، ح ١٠١١

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.en:
Ammar narrated:  
"Whoever gives the salutation (taslim) after two rak'ahs of the Dhuhr, Asr, Maghrib, or 
Isha prayers, and then remembers, should continue with their prayer, even if they 
reach China, and there is no need for them to repeat it”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 347 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 962 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 1012

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1014  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ إ َّن ُّو كَّهْمَّسٍ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ِ إَّلز َّكْعَّي َّي ْن ِ إَّلْأ ُّولَّي َّي ْن ِ ق َّا ِذ َّإ ج َّلَّسْث ُّ ق ِي هِمَّا لِلي  َّس َّه ُّذِ ق َّق ُّلْث ُّ وَّ 
إ َّن َّا ج َّالِس  إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّلَّي ْك َّ إ َّي  ُّهَّا إَّلن  َّن ِى  ُّ وَّ رَّجْمَّه ُّ إَّلل َّهِ وَّ ن َّزَّكَّاي ُّهُّ إِن ْصِزَّإف   هُّ وَّق َّالَّ  «
عِن َّاذِ إَّلل َّهِ إَّلص َّالِحِي ن َّ ق َّهُّوَّ إِن ْصِزَّإف .
hadiths.en:
Ubayd ibn Zurarah asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} about a 
man who prays the Fajr prayer, offering one rak'ah, then makes the tashahhud 
(testification), turns away (ends the prayer), and leaves. After some time, he 
remembers that he only prayed one rak'ah. He asked what should he do.  
Imam {a.s} replied: "He should add another rak'ah to it”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 1013

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1015  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى  ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال ث َّ إ َّمْ إ َّرْن َّعاً وَّ لَّمْ ي َّذ ْهَّث ْ  
وَّهْمُّك َّ إ ِلَّى س َّى ْءٍ ق َّي َّس َّه َّذْ وَّ سَّلِّمْ ي ُّم َّ صَّلِّ رَّكْعَّي َّي ْن ِ وَّ إ َّرْن َّغَّ سَّح َّذَّإب ٍ ي َّق ْ زَّإ ُّ ق ِي هِمَّا ن ِا ُّمِّ إَّلْكِي َّاب ِ ي ُّم َّ ي َّس َّه َّذُّ وَّ ي ُّسَّلِّمُّ ق َّا ِن ْ 
كُّن ْث َّ إ ِن  َّمَّا صَّل َّي ْث َّ رَّكْعَّي َّي ْن ِ كَّان َّي َّا هَّاي َّان ِ ي َّمَّامَّ إَّلْأ َّرْن َّغِ وَّ إ ِن ْ كُّن ْث َّ صَّل َّي ْث َّ إ َّرْن َّعاً كَّان َّي َّا هَّا ي َّان ِ ن َّاق ِلَّه ً .
hadiths.en:
Abu Kahmas asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} about the two 
rak'ahs of prayer when, after sitting in the tashahhud position, he says:  
(Peace be upon you, O’ Prophet, and the mercy of Allah {SWT} and His blessings). Is this 
considered the ending (of the prayer)?  
Imam {a.s} replied: "No, it is not the ending. But when you say 'As-Salamu Alayna wa Ala 
Ibadillah As -Saliheen' (Peace be upon us and upon the righteous servants of Allah 
{SWT}), that is the ending."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 348 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 316 • Al-Sara’ir, Part 
3, p. 604 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 2, p. 41 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 773 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 426 • Bihar Al -
Anwar, Vol. 82, p. 309 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 1014

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1016  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ج َّمِي لُّ ن ْن ُّ ذَّر َّإح ٍ عَّن ْهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال إَّلز َّإن ِعَّه ِ مِق ْذَّإرَّ  
إَّلي  َّس َّه ُّذِ ق َّعِن َّاذَّي ُّهُّ ج َّان ِز َّة   .
hadiths.en:
Al-Halabi narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} that He said:  
"If you do not know whether you prayed two rak'ahs or four, and your doubt does not 
lead you to any conclusion, then perform the tashahhud and give the salaam, then 
pray two more rak'ahs with four prostrations. In them, recite Al -Fatiha.  
Afterward, perform the tashahhud and give the salaam. If you had prayed only two 
rak'ahs, then these will complete the four. But if you had prayed four, then these two 
will be considered as voluntary (nawafil)." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 349 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 219 • Mustadrak Al -
Wasail, Vol. 6, p. 410 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 1015

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1017  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْعَّلاَّءُّ عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ مُّسْلِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ: سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ْ رَّج لٍ صَّل َّى إَّلط  ُّهْزَّ ج َّمْساً  
ق َّق َّال ق َّلْي َّح ْعَّلْ إ َّرْن َّغَّ رَّكَّعَّاب ٍ مِن ْهَّا إَّلط  ُّهْزَّ وَّ ي َّح ْلِسُّ وَّ ي َّي َّس َّه َّذُّ ي ُّم َّ 
ي ُّصَّلِّى  وَّ هُّوَّ ج َّالِس  رَّكْعَّي َّي ْن ِ وَّ إ َّرْن َّغَّ سَّح َّذَّإب ٍ ق َّي ُّص ِي ق ُّهُّمَّا إ ِلَّى إَّلْح َّامِسَّه ِ ق َّي َّكُّون ُّ ن َّاق ِلَّه ً .
hadiths.en:
Jamil ibn Darraj narrated from him {a.s} that He said: "If a man prays five rak'ahs 
and sits in the fourth rak'ah for the duration of the tashahhud, then his worship is 
valid."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 349 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 965 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 1016

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1018  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ إَّلْق ُّص َّي ْلُّ ن ْن ُّ ي َّسَّارٍ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ِ إَّلس َّهْوِ ق َّق َّال 
سَّح ْذَّي َّا إَّلس َّهْوِ وَّ إ ِن  َّمَّا إَّلس َّهْوُّ عَّلَّى مَّن ْ لَّمْ ي َّذْرِ إ َّ ر َّإذَّ ق ِى  صَّلاَّي ِهِ إ َّمْ ن َّق َّضَّ مِن ْهَّا .
hadiths.en:
Al-Ala reported from Muhammad ibn Muslim from Imam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} who said:  
"I asked him about a man who prayed Dhuhr five rak'ahs. He said: 'If he does not know 
whether he sat in the fourth rak'ah or not, let him treat it as four rak'ahs for the Dhuhr 
prayer, then he should sit, perform the tashahhud, and then pray two rak'ahs wh ile 
sitting and perform four sajdahs. He should then add these to the fifth rak'ah, and it 
will be considered a nafl (voluntary) prayer.'" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 349 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 965 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 
233 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 85, p. 202 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 1017

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1019  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى  ُّ عَّن ْهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال ر ِذْب َّ إ َّمْ ن َّق َّصْث َّ ق َّي َّس َّه َّذْ  
وَّ سَّلِّمْ وَّ إُّسْح ُّذْ سَّح ْذَّي َّى ِ إَّلس َّهْوِ ن ِع َّي ْزِ رُّكُّوعٍ وَّ لأَّ ق ِزَّإءَّة ٍ ي َّي َّس َّه َّذُّ ق ِي هِمَّا ي َّس َّه ُّذإً ج َّق ِي ق اً .
hadiths.en:
Al-Fudail ibn Yasar asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} about 
forgetfulness in prayer.  
Imam said: "Whoever remembers his forgetfulness and corrects it, there is no need for 
him to perform the two prostrations of forgetfulness (Sajdatay al -Sahw). However, 
forgetfulness applies to the one who does not know whether he has added to or 
missed part of his prayer."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 1018

reference.location: Volume 1 › THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٩، ح ١٠١٨

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.en:
Al-Halabi narrated from him (the Prophet {a.s}) that He said:  
“If you are unsure whether you prayed four or five rak'ahs, or whether you added or 
omitted something, then perform the tashahhud (testification of faith), say salaam 
(greeting of peace), and prostrate two prostrations of forgetfulness without bowing or 
reciting (in them), and you will make a brief tashahhud in both of them.”  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 350 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 196 • Al -Istibsar, 
Part 1, p. 380 • Nuzhat al -Nāẓir, Part 1, p. 40 • Awaali al -La’aali, Part 3, p. 105 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 988 • 
Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 224 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 234 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 85, p. 206 • Bihar Al -
Anwar, Vol. 85, p. 226 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 1019
reference.chapter: THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٩، ح ١٠١٩

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1021  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّن ْذُّ إَّلز َّجْمَّن ِ ن ْن ُّ إَّلْحَّح  َّاح ِ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  إ ِن ْزَّإهِي مَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ: ق ُّلْث ُّ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل هِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ، 
رَّج ُّل  لأَّ ي َّذْرِى  إ َّ ي ِن ْي َّي ْن ِ صَّل َّى إ َّمْ ي َّلاَّي اً إ َّمْ إ َّرْن َّعاً ق َّق َّالَّ
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated that Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} 
was asked about a man who entered the prayer with the Imam and was already behind 
by one rak'ah.  
When the Imam completed the prayer and left with the congregation, the man 
remembered that he had missed one rak'ah.  
Imam {a.s} said: "He should repeat one rak'ah."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 350 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 405 • 
Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 346 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 367 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 198 • Wasail Al-Shia, 
Part 8, p. 202 
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
« .

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 1020
reference.chapter: THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER

hadiths.chapter: 49. THE RULES OF FORGETFULNESS (SAHW) IN PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1022   -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ عَّلِى ِّ ن ْن ِ إ َّن ِى  جَّمْز َّة َّ عَّن ِ إَّلْعَّن ْذِ إَّلص َّالِحِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ: سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ   ي َّس ُّك  ُّ ق َّلاَّ ي َّذْرِى  إ َّ  
وَّإجِذَّة ً صَّل َّى إ َّوِ إِي ْن َّي َّي ْن ِ إ َّوْ ي َّلاَّي اً إ َّوْ إ َّرْن َّعاً ي َّلْي َّن ِسُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ صَّلاَّي ُّهُّ ق َّق َّال ل ُّ ذ َّإ  
ق َّق ُّلْث ُّ ن َّعَّمْ ق َّال إَّلس  َّي ْطَّان ِ إَّلز َّج ِي مِ ق َّا ِن  َّهُّ ي ُّوس ِك ُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّذ ْهَّث َّ عَّن ْهُّ .
hadiths.en:
Abdurrahman ibn Al-Hajjaj narrated from Abu Ibrahim who said:  
"I asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} about a man who does not know 
whether he prayed two, three, or four rak'ahs.  
Imam {a.s} said: 'He should pray two rak'ahs standing, then make salaam (salutation), 
then pray two more rak'ahs while sitting.' 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 350 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 981 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 8, p. 
222 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 85, p. 184 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 49, H. 1021
